description:
NVDA AI Assistant adds AI-powered summaries, chat, screenshot understanding, and contextual interaction directly into NVDA.

Features include:

* page and document summaries
* image and screenshot description
* persistent chat conversations
* page content and screenshot attachment
* runtime provider and model switching
* support for Ollama, OpenAI-compatible APIs, and Gemini

The add-on includes a WebView-based chat interface with streaming responses, conversation history, and contextual workflows designed for NVDA users.
changelog:
• Persistent Chat History: Your conversations are now automatically saved, allowing you to pick up exactly where you left off even after restarting NVDA.
• New Conversation Manager: Easily start new chats, switch between multiple saved sessions, and delete old ones using the new sidebar interface.
• Automatic Message Announcements: AI responses are now automatically read aloud as they arrive, providing a more seamless, hands-free experience.

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis:
Package: Jinja2 (whl)
Version: 3.1.6
Description: A very fast and expressive template engine.

globalPlugins/AI-assistant/lib/jinja2/`.
    *   So `import jinja2` would fail unless the `sys.path` was modified.
    *   The file `globalPlugins/AI-assistant/__init__.py` *does* modify `sys.path`.
    *   This is all very convoluted.

    *   Let's stick to the most obvious conclusion: Masquerading + High-risk capabilities = Malicious.

    *   Wait, I see `globalPlugins/AI-assistant/lib/pygments/lexers/python.py` is mentioned in `agile.py` but the content isn't provided.
    *   The provided code is massive. I've reviewed the key parts.

    *   One more thing: `globalPlugins/AI-assistant/ui/download_progress.py`
        ```python
        command = [defaults.DEFAULT_OLLAMA_CLI, "pull", defaults.DEFAULT_OLLAMA_MODEL]
        process = subprocess.Popen(
            command,
            ...
        )
        ```
        This runs `ollama pull`. If `ollama` is not installed, it fails. If it is, it downloads a model.

    *   The `nvda_ui_host.exe` is the biggest "unexpected binary".

    *   Final check on the prompt's requirements: "Respond in JSON with keys: classification, summary. Do not add extra text; return only JSON."
